title: potentilla subacaulis var. incana
intro: Potentilla subacaulis var. incana, also known as Hoary Five-finger, is a shrubby perennial plant species within the rose family that is native to North America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The plant's leaves, roots, and stems have been traditionally used to treat conditions such as sore throats, fever, and skin irritations, offering health benefits like reduced inflammation and antimicrobial protection. Hoary Five-finger can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms of traditional medicine.
